issue.title: [BOUNTY $30] WORKFLOW: OpenClaw or Hermes integration
issue.body:
**Bounty:** $30 (LT)

REPO: https://github.com/lobster-trap/TentOfTrials

Integrate OpenClaw or Hermes into the repo by creating a script to launch either with the repo context already loaded.

Acceptance criteria:
- Create script to launch OpenClaw or Hermes (of your choosing) covering: project purpose, tech stack per directory, common dev commands, coding conventions, and known pitfalls
- Must include the build-and-diagnostic workflow: "Always run `python3 build.py` after changes and include the diagnostic artifacts in your PR"
- Include a "Where to start" section for each module (which file to read first)
- The tone should match the repo's existing self-deprecating style (read README.md and a few source files for reference)
- No markdown lint errors (run through a linter or validate manually)

Required validation:
- Run `python3 build.py`
- Include the generated diagnostic `.logd` artifact from `diagnostic/build-XXX.logd` in the PR notes; also include `diagnostic/build-XXX.json` if present
- You must use `.github/pull_request_template.md` for your submission

issue.title: [$25 BOUNTY] [Python] Add independent log parser fixtures
issue.body:
**Bounty:** $25 (LT)

**Area:** `tools/log_aggregator.py` and parser fixture tests

**Current state:** The legacy log aggregator notes that parser tests can false-pass because the test data was generated by the same parser logic. This leaves JSON, text, and nginx log parsing vulnerable to regressions when real archived log formats drift.

**What's needed:** Add independent parser fixtures based on hand-written representative log lines and cover the main parser paths without relying on parser-generated samples. Keep the change focused on parser validation and do not change production parsing behavior unless a fixture exposes a clear bug.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Add independent fixtures for JSON, plain text, and nginx-style log lines.
- Add focused tests or a validation script that verifies parser output for timestamp, level, service/format, and key fields.
- Include at least one malformed/unsupported line case that should not crash parsing.
- Existing log aggregation CLI behavior remains compatible.
- PR description explains the fixture source/intent and validation performed.

**Required validation:**
- Run `python3 build.py`
- Include the generated diagnostic `.logd` artifact from `diagnostic/build-XXX.logd` in the PR; also include `diagnostic/build-XXX.json` if present
- Use `.github/pull_request_template.md` for your submission

issue.title: [$50 BOUNTY] [Python] Add cross-platform health check fallbacks
issue.body:
**Bounty:** $50 (LT)

**Area:** `tools/health_check.py`

**Current state:** The health check tool reads Linux-only `/proc/meminfo` and `/proc/loadavg` directly for memory and load checks. On macOS or other non-Linux environments those checks degrade to warning-only results even though Python standard-library fallbacks can still provide useful diagnostics.

**What's needed:** Add cross-platform fallbacks for the memory and load checks so non-Linux environments report useful values instead of generic `/proc` read failures. Keep the Linux behavior compatible with the current output.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `check_memory_usage()` keeps the existing `/proc/meminfo` path on Linux but has a non-Linux fallback that returns a meaningful status/detail tuple.
- `check_load_average()` keeps the existing `/proc/loadavg` path where available but falls back to `os.getloadavg()` or another standard-library path where possible.
- Add focused tests or a small validation path that simulates missing `/proc` files and verifies fallback behavior.
- JSON and text health-check output remain backward compatible for existing callers.
- PR description explains the fallback behavior and validation performed.

**Required validation:**
- Run `python3 build.py`
- Include the generated diagnostic `.logd` artifact from `diagnostic/build-XXX.logd` in the PR; also include `diagnostic/build-XXX.json` if present
- Use `.github/pull_request_template.md` for your submission

issue.title: [$30 BOUNTY] [Python] Fix HighMemoryUsage alert ratio expression
issue.body:
**Bounty:** $30 (LT)

**Area:** `tools/monitoring_setup.py`

**Current state:** The recommended `HighMemoryUsage` Prometheus alert currently uses `process_resident_memory_bytes / process_resident_memory_bytes > 0.9`, which is self-dividing and always evaluates to `1` for any nonzero value. That makes the alert rule unreliable and prevents it from reflecting real memory pressure.

**What's needed:** Replace the self-dividing memory expression with a real memory-usage ratio that compares process resident memory against an appropriate total/available memory metric, and add validation so this regression is covered. Keep the change scoped to the monitoring setup path.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `HighMemoryUsage` no longer divides `process_resident_memory_bytes` by itself.
- The replacement expression uses a meaningful denominator and remains valid PromQL syntax.
- Add or update a focused test or validation path that catches self-dividing alert expressions.
- Existing monitoring setup behavior and dry-run output remain compatible.
- PR description explains the alert expression change and validation performed.

**Required validation:**
- Run `python3 build.py`
- Include the generated diagnostic `.logd` artifact from `diagnostic/build-XXX.logd` in the PR; also include `diagnostic/build-XXX.json` if present
- Use `.github/pull_request_template.md` for your submission

issue.title: [NEW SKILL] geo-redundant-secret-replication-review
issue.body:
## Proposed Skill
**Skill name:** `geo-redundant-secret-replication-review`
**Category:** secrets
**Severity:** high

## What It Detects
Cross-region secret replication may expand decryption scope or weaken revocation if replication and residency controls are not explicit.

## Why This Skill Is Needed
This topic appears in real security reviews, but it is not represented cleanly in the current library. A dedicated skill would make the review repeatable and easier to apply across products.

## Detection Approach
Map the trust boundary, identify where authority is derived, then review validation, provenance, exception handling, replay behavior, and background or operator paths that may get broader reach than intended.

## Languages / Frameworks
- secret managers
- multi-region infrastructure

## Example Vulnerable Pattern
```text
Authority or access is inferred from a weak context signal,
then reused in a broader path without a fresh authorization check.
```

## Example Remediation
```text
Bind authority to explicit actor and resource context,
re-check it at the sensitive boundary,
and log approval or provenance for privileged paths.
```

## References
- OWASP ASVS
- NIST SP 800-53
- Relevant vendor or protocol guidance for this control family

## Estimated Complexity
- [ ] **Standard** ($200) - Well-known vuln class, single language, straightforward detection
- [x] **Intermediate** ($350) - Multiple languages/frameworks, nuanced detection logic
- [ ] **Complex** ($500) - Novel detection approach, comprehensive coverage, low FP rate

## Bounty Info
- [x] I have read and agree to the [CONTRIBUTING.md](../../CONTRIBUTING.md) bounty terms
- **Preferred payment method:** PayPal https://paypal.me/Jeremytsangchina

comment.body:
Submitted PR #2577 for this item: https://github.com/UnitOneAI/SecuritySkills/pull/2577

It adds the `geo-redundant-secret-replication-review` skill, updates the index, and includes vulnerable/benign fixtures for AWS, GCP, and Vault-style DR replication patterns. Local validation passed for frontmatter, index references, prompt-injection scan, and diff whitespace.

issue.title: MRWK bounty: 40 MRWK - review open MergeWork PRs with evidence, round 18

labels.name: repo-3bade996
labels.description: Code review and PR review work

issue.body:
This is a proposal-gated review bounty issue. It is not claimable yet. Do not submit `/claim` work from this issue until the treasury proposal executes, the public bounty page exists, and maintainers post `Reserved on MergeWork`.

## Work needed
Review open MergeWork pull requests with concrete current-head evidence. This round is proposed early so review capacity is ready before the current review round empties.

## Reward
40 MRWK per accepted review, up to 30 awards, if the treasury proposal executes.

## Priority targets
- Maintainer infrastructure PRs that are clean, mergeable, and close to acceptance.
- PRs that affect treasury, bounty lifecycle, admin flow, deployment, documentation, or queue safety.
- Blocker reviews on stale-looking or dirty PRs where the review gives maintainers a clear close/fix/rebase decision.

## Acceptance criteria
Accepted reviews must be by non-authors and must link a GitHub PR review or concise PR comment with concrete current-head evidence. Useful evidence includes files inspected, behavior checked, commands run, CI reviewed, mergeability checked, duplicate/overlap check, current-head SHA reviewed, or a specific actionable finding.

## Out of scope
- rubber-stamp approvals
- vague comments
- stale-head reviews that do not account for current code
- duplicate reviews that add no new evidence
- self-reviews
- reviews that treat closed, exhausted, or pending-only bounty issues as live work
- reviews containing private secrets, private vulnerability details, or speculative MRWK price, exchange, bridge, liquidity, cash-out, or payout claims

## Duplicate and stale review rules
Duplicate work is judged by the first useful, evidence-backed review. Stale reviews may be skipped if the PR head changes materially before acceptance. Reviews may identify blockers and still qualify if they give maintainers concrete action.

comment.body:
/claim #654

Review submitted for PR #738: https://github.com/ramimbo/mergework/pull/738#pullrequestreview-4406132942

Evidence summary:
- Reviewed current head `5ac3cff218140589ba908ac1adf88a731e00b661` against current `upstream/main`.
- Found a current-head MCP input-schema/runtime-parser contract mismatch: schema accepts padded / plus-prefixed integer strings while `MCP_INTEGER_RE.fullmatch(value)` rejects them. Requested changes with remediation guidance.
- `git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main pr-738` succeeded.
- `python3 -m pytest tests/test_api_mcp.py tests/test_docs_public_urls.py -q` -> `114 passed`.
- docs smoke, Ruff check, Ruff format check, and `git diff --check` passed.

This is a differentiated current-head review after the previous conflict-only blocker; no private data, wallet material, payout claim, exchange, bridge, or cash-out guidance is included.

issue.title: MRWK bounty: 50 MRWK - accepted proposed-work requests, round 1
issue.body:
## MRWK Bounty

Status: proposed bounty. This issue is not claimable until the treasury proposal executes, the public bounty page exists, and the `Reserved on MergeWork` claims-open comment is posted.

Reward: `50 MRWK per accepted award`
Max awards: `20`

## Work Needed

Open useful proposed-work issues using the proposed-work issue template. Accepted proposals should help maintainers choose, size, or reject future work without guessing.

## How The Process Works

1. Open a new issue with the `Proposed work` template.
2. Link this bounty issue from that proposed-work issue, or comment here with the proposed-work issue URL.
3. Include the problem, current evidence, proposed work, expected value, acceptance or test notes, duplicate search, out-of-scope notes, and a suggested reference tier if useful.
4. Maintainers review the proposed-work issue. Accepted proposal posts can be paid from this bounty through a public `pay_bounty` proposal.
5. The proposed-work issue itself is not a live implementation bounty unless a separate `create_bounty` proposal later executes and adds `mrwk:bounty` plus `Reserved on MergeWork`.

## Acceptance Criteria

A proposal qualifies only when it is concrete, current, non-duplicate, and useful for the project. It should include enough evidence for maintainers or agents to understand the problem, why it matters, and what accepted future work could look like.

Useful proposals usually include:

- links to the relevant docs, code, UI route, API route, issue, PR, discussion, or observed behavior
- a clear maintainer decision the proposal supports
- rough acceptance criteria or verification steps for future work
- duplicate search notes
- clear non-goals and out-of-scope work
- a suggested README reference tier when sizing guidance helps

Reference tiers are guidance only. They do not make a proposed-work issue claimable and do not promise payment for future implementation.

## Evidence Required

For payment review, link the proposed-work issue and summarize why it is useful. The proposed-work issue must contain the concrete evidence, duplicate search, and scope notes above.

## Out Of Scope

- Vague idea lists, generic AI output, or broad rewrites without evidence.
- Duplicate proposals for the same problem or work already covered by a live or pending bounty.
- No-value typo-only posts unless they identify a real documentation failure pattern.
- Claims that a proposed-work issue is already a live bounty.
- Price, investment, exchange, liquidity, bridge, cash-out, fabricated payout, private security detail, secret, or token claims.

## Duplicate And Stale Work Rules

Duplicate work is judged by the first useful, reviewable proposed-work issue that identifies the problem and gives maintainers enough evidence to act. Maintainers may reject stale, superseded, unsupported, or no-longer-relevant proposals.

issue.title: Proposed work: expose active-attempt summary on bounty API rows

labels.name: repo-3bade996
labels.description: Proposed work intake, not a live MRWK bounty

issue.body:
## Problem

MergeWork exposes advisory attempt reservations through
`GET /api/v1/bounties/{id}/attempts`, but the public bounty list and detail
responses do not include an active-attempt summary.

That creates an avoidable N+1 lookup problem for agents scanning several live
bounties. An agent can see open award capacity in `/api/v1/bounties`, select a
task, and miss overlapping work unless it remembers to fetch a second endpoint
for every candidate. This increases duplicate PR risk during busy multi-agent
rounds.

The attempts endpoint should remain the detailed source of truth. The missing
piece is a small summary signal on bounty rows so scanners know when a detailed
attempt lookup is especially important.

## Evidence

Read-only checks on 2026-05-31:

- Live bounty #649: https://github.com/ramimbo/mergework/issues/649
- Public bounty detail: https://api.mrwk.online/api/v1/bounties/96
- Public attempts detail: https://api.mrwk.online/api/v1/bounties/96/attempts
- The bounty detail response includes `awards_remaining`,
  `effective_awards_remaining`, `availability_state`, and
  `availability_note`, but no `active_attempt_count`,
  `active_attempt_warnings`, or equivalent summary.
- The attempts response separately returns `bounty_id`, `warnings`, and
  `attempts`.
- `docs/agent-guide.md` already tells agents to inspect active attempts before
  opening overlapping work.
- `docs/api-examples.md` documents the attempts endpoint and warns agents to
  pick a different scope when another attempt already covers the work.
- `app.serializers.bounty_to_dict` serializes award availability but does not
  expose an attempt-summary field.
- `app.bounty_attempts.bounty_attempt_warnings` already centralizes advisory
  warning logic, so a summary can reuse existing behavior rather than inventing
  a second rule set.

Observed live response for bounty id `96` at check time:

```json
{
  "status": "open",
  "awards_remaining": 20,
  "effective_awards_remaining": 20,
  "availability_state": "open"
}
```

The separate attempts response was:

```json
{
  "bounty_id": 96,
  "warnings": [],
  "attempts": []
}
```

## Proposed work

Expose a bounded active-attempt summary on public bounty list/detail responses
and agent-facing structured guidance.

Smallest useful response shape:

```json
{
  "active_attempt_count": 2,
  "active_attempt_warnings": ["bounty has 2 active attempts"],
  "attempt_endpoint": "/api/v1/bounties/96/attempts"
}
```

Keep full reservation rows, submitter accounts, source URLs, expiration times,
and released/expired audit history in the existing attempts endpoint. The
summary should stay advisory and read-only.

If list-query cost is a concern, maintainer choice can limit the first slice to
single-bounty detail plus MCP guidance, or use a bounded/preloaded count for the
list serializer.

## Expected value

- Helps agents notice overlap before coding or opening duplicate PRs.
- Reduces N+1 requests for the common scanner decision: "does this bounty
  already have active attempts?"
- Keeps detailed attempt inspection available when a warning exists.
- Makes public API and MCP guidance better aligned with the repository's
  documented duplicate-avoidance workflow.
- Does not block contributors or reserve payment slots; attempts remain
  advisory hints only.

## Reference tier

100-500 MRWK: useful issue, test, docs page, small bugfix

## Possible acceptance criteria

- `GET /api/v1/bounties/{id}` exposes a bounded active-attempt summary.
- Maintainers decide whether `GET /api/v1/bounties` also exposes the summary in
  the first slice or in a follow-up optimized slice.
- The summary reuses existing active-attempt status and warning semantics.
- Expired and released attempts do not inflate the active count.
- Closed, paid, and exhausted bounty behavior remains unchanged.
- The detailed `/api/v1/bounties/{id}/attempts` response remains available for
  reservation rows and audit history.
- MCP or docs guidance points agents to the detailed endpoint when the summary
  reports overlap.

## Evidence or tests required

- Serializer/API tests for zero, one, multiple, expired, and released attempts.
- Regression test that expired attempts do not count as active.
- Regression test that detailed attempt rows remain available through the
  existing endpoint.
- MCP structured-guidance test if that surface is updated.
- `ruff`, `mypy`, focused `pytest`, and docs smoke checks for touched files.

## Duplicate search

Checked current related issues and PRs on 2026-05-31:

- #676 proposes structured submission mode for non-PR bounties, not overlap
  summaries.
- #672 proposes a proposed-work intake triage report, not bounty attempt
  visibility.
- #671 and #673 cover pending accepted-work visibility, not pre-work overlap
  reservations.
- #641 covers effective award availability after pending payout/close proposals,
  not advisory contributor attempts.
- #645 and PR #660 cover non-live bounty references in PR queue health, not
  active-attempt summaries on public bounty API rows.
- Existing attempt API docs and tests cover the detailed endpoint, but I did not
  find an open proposal for a bounded summary on bounty list/detail rows.

## Out of scope

- No changes to treasury execution, balances, proofs, payout rules, award
  capacity, or acceptance decisions.
- No automatic blocking of contributors when another attempt exists.
- No exposure of private session data or secret material.
- No removal of the detailed attempts endpoint.
- No broad serializer redesign.
- No price, investment, exchange, liquidity, bridge, cash-out, fabricated
  payout, private security detail, or secret claims.

Related bounty for review: #649

issue.title: MRWK bounty: 40 MRWK - review open MergeWork PRs with evidence, round 16

labels.name: repo-3bade996
labels.description: Code review and PR review work

issue.body:
## Proposed MRWK Bounty

Reward: `40 MRWK per accepted award`
Max awards: `17`

Status: proposed, not yet reserved. This issue must not receive `mrwk:bounty` or `Reserved on MergeWork` until its treasury proposal executes and the public bounty row exists.

## Work Needed

Review open MergeWork PRs with specific evidence. Focus on duplicates, overlap, mergeability, failing checks, stale bounty references, security/public-copy risks, whether the change is actually useful, and whether it might conflict with active maintainer branches.

Round 15 is #578. This proposed round should only become active after the reserve proposal executes. The award count is 17 to fit the current 24-hour treasury reserve cap.

## Acceptance Criteria

Accepted review claims must:

- Be by someone other than the PR author.
- Link a GitHub PR review or concise PR comment.
- Include concrete evidence: files inspected, behavior checked, commands run, CI status reviewed, mergeability checked, or an actionable finding.
- Be distinct from already paid or pending-proposal review claims.
- Help maintain the project rule that PRs need at least two useful reviews before maintainer acceptance.

Duplicate, vague, self-review, rubber-stamp, misleading, stale-head, or superseded duplicate reviews do not qualify.

## How To Submit

Wait for the maintainer `Reserved on MergeWork` comment before submitting `/claim` work for MRWK. After reserve execution, comment here with `/claim` and link the PR review or concise PR comment.

## Evidence or Tests Required

Include the PR number, what you inspected, and the commands or checks you ran. If you request changes, state the blocker clearly and avoid private vulnerability details.

## Out of Scope

Do not post private keys, seed material, OAuth tokens, cookies, admin tokens, private vulnerability details, price claims, investment claims, liquidity claims, exchange claims, bridge promises, off-ramp promises, or fabricated payout claims.

## Duplicate and Stale Work Rules

The first useful review of a distinct issue is preferred. Repeating the same finding without new evidence may be skipped. Reviews of stale, duplicate, or superseded PRs only qualify when the review identifies a real actionable issue or a clear reason to close, rebase, or hold the PR.

comment.body:
/claim #601

Reviewed PR: https://github.com/ramimbo/mergework/pull/664
Head commit: `816d15008e3b58c959211a67a1b6c86e886c2d38`

Submitted a current-head approval review with concrete evidence: inspected the focused `docs/agent-guide.md` diff, checked status-language correctness and public-artifact hygiene, verified green GitHub checks and CodeRabbit output, ran `python3 scripts/docs_smoke.py` (`docs smoke ok`), ran `git diff --check upstream/main...upstream/pr-664` (clean), and ran `git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main upstream/pr-664` (clean tree `6baa3482836080343a817eca3024afad142e54af`).

This is distinct from the earlier review and from skipped PR-template wording work.

review.body:
Reviewed current head `816d15008e3b58c959211a67a1b6c86e886c2d38` for the live review bounty #601.

Verdict: no blocker found in this focused docs-only change.

Evidence:
- Inspected the only changed file: `docs/agent-guide.md` (+18 lines).
- Confirmed the new section distinguishes Submitted, Accepted, Pending payout, and Paid without overstating payout state.
- Confirmed paid wording is tied to an exact public proof or ledger entry and explicitly avoids cash-value, exchangeability, bridge, off-ramp, and fabricated payout claims.
- Confirmed PR #664 is distinct from the PR-template wording work that was skipped in #663: this change targets agent-facing status language.
- Reviewed GitHub checks: Quality/readiness/docs/image checks passed; CodeRabbit reported no actionable comments.
- Ran `python3 scripts/docs_smoke.py` -> `docs smoke ok`.
- Ran `git diff --check upstream/main...upstream/pr-664` -> clean.
- Ran `git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main upstream/pr-664` -> clean tree `6baa3482836080343a817eca3024afad142e54af`.

issue.title: [300 MRG per PR] Test submitted PRs and verify bounty evidence

labels.name: mergeos
labels.description: Quality assurance, regression testing, and verification work.

issue.body:
## Summary

We need reliable QA verification for submitted bounty PRs. This bounty pays **300 MRG per accepted PR verification**.

A verification may be completed by a human tester or an automated agent, as long as the final report is clear, reproducible, and includes evidence.

## Bounty

- Reward: **300 MRG per PR verified**
- Bounty type: QA / testing / evidence review
- Area: open bounty pull requests
- Eligible work: testing a submitted PR, checking the required build/test results, and validating the PR evidence

## Scope

Pick an open bounty PR and verify whether it is actually ready for bounty review.

For each tested PR, the verifier should:

1. Identify the target PR number, author, branch, and head commit SHA.
2. Check GitHub Actions status for the PR.
3. Run the relevant local checks when possible, for example:
   - `npm test`
   - `npm run build:local`
   - backend Go tests/builds when backend behavior changed
4. Manually test the feature or bug fix described by the linked bounty issue.
5. Check that the PR author attached the required evidence, such as screenshots, video/GIF, console/network logs, sandbox proof, or build/test output.
6. Confirm whether the evidence matches the issue requirements and the actual PR behavior.
7. Comment a concise verification report on the target PR.
8. Comment back on this issue with a link to the verification report.

## Verification Report Requirements

Each accepted report must include:

- Target PR link and head commit SHA.
- Commands run and pass/fail result.
- GitHub Actions result at the time of review.
- Manual test summary.
- Evidence status: provided, missing, incomplete, or invalid.
- Screenshots, short video/GIF, logs, or terminal output when relevant.
- Final recommendation: approve, request changes, or needs maintainer review.

## Evidence Rules

A PR should not be marked ready if evidence is missing or does not prove the claimed behavior.

For UI/responsive work, evidence should include the required viewports from the linked bounty issue.

For payment, webhook, sandbox, backend, or security-sensitive work, evidence should include safe-to-share logs, callback results, sandbox configuration notes, ledger/admin/proof state, and relevant test output.

## Duplicate Work Rules

- Do not verify your own PR.
- Only one accepted verification report is paid per target PR unless a maintainer asks for a retest after new commits.
- If multiple people test the same PR, the first complete and useful report is prioritized.
- Low-effort comments without commands, evidence, or a clear verdict are not eligible.

## Claim Requirement

Before starting, comment on this issue with the PR number you want to verify.

After posting the verification report on the target PR, comment here with:

- Target PR number
- Link to your verification report comment
- Your payout address

comment.body:
/attempt #64

**QA Verification: PR #108** — fix: feat: project category selection step (Bounty #12)

**Target PR:** https://github.com/mergeos-bounties/mergeos/pull/108
**Author:** sriraajj-lab
**Head commit:** `f0808573496086c916d6b93e695e9162d96b0679`
**Linked bounty:** #12 / #65

**1. CI Status:**
- Secret scan: success
- Backend build and test: success
- Web build and test (frontend): success
- Web build and test (admin): success
- Web build and test (scan): success
- Mergeable: true

**2. Local Verification:**
Fresh checkout of PR #108 under `frontend/`:

```bash
npm ci
npm test
npm run build:local
```

Results:
- `npm test`: 8/8 tests passed
- `npm run build:local`: client build passed, SSR build passed

**3. Code Review / Functional Finding:**
- Diff: 1 file, `frontend/src/App.vue`, +45/-10
- The PR changes the wizard to start at `projectWizardStep = 0` and adds a `projectSetupSteps` entry for `Project category`.
- However, the template still only renders the setup form/help/rail card at `projectWizardStep === 1`.
- There is no rendered category picker for step 0.
- `selectProjectCategory(category)` is defined but not referenced in the template, so users cannot select either project category.
- `nextProjectStep()` can move from step 0 to step 1, but that bypasses the intended category selection and leaves `projectCategory` empty.

Evidence from template/code search:
- `projectWizardStep === 1` controls the visible setup form.
- `selectProjectCategory` only appears in the script definition, not in template usage.

**4. Quality Assessment:**
- ✅ CI and local tests/build pass.
- ✅ Scope is small and single-file.
- ❌ Main bounty behavior is incomplete: the new category selection step is not visible/interactable.
- ❌ The new category state is not actually connected to a rendered UI.

**Verdict:** REQUEST CHANGES — not ready for bounty acceptance until step 0 renders a real category selection UI and wires the choices to `selectProjectCategory()`.

**Payout address:** `github:weilixiong`
